pr.title: Label misc dolphin SDK symbols missed in previous passes
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Labeled a broad set of previously anonymous Dolphin SDK assembly symbols across OdemuExi2/DebuggerDriver, HIO, MCC, VI, and related call sites. The PR mostly replaced address-based func_/lbl_ names with SDK-style function and data names, propagated those names through branch/call references, and adjusted a few data declarations and alignment boundaries so newly named symbols describe the intended storage units. There was no PR body or review discussion; the only recorded review was an approval.

change_classification.primary_type: assembly symbol labeling and data layout cleanup

what_changed: Renamed DebuggerDriver function and callback labels such as func_8032B4E0 to DBGReadStatus, lbl_8032B58C to MWCallback, and lbl_8032B5C8 to DBGHandler.
Renamed DebuggerDriver small-data globals from lbl_ addresses to semantic names including MTRCallback, DBGCallback, SendMailData, RecvDataLeng, pEXIInputFlag, EXIInputFlag, and SendCount.
Renamed HIO state and callback globals such as Dev, TxCallback, and RxCallback, and labeled async entry points HIOReadAsync and HIOWriteAsync.
Renamed MCC functions func_8032D88C, func_8032D954, and func_8032D9AC to MCCExit, MCCEnumDevices, and MCCGetFreeBlocks, and updated callers in MCC and sysdolphin baselib.
Renamed the large MCC bss block lbl_804A50E0 to gChannelInfo and split its contents into named trailing regions gStreamWork, m_szAdapterMode, m_szInitCode, and channelInfo.
Renamed VI function func_803503A8 to VIGetDTVStatus and updated a melee/text_2.s call site to use VIGetDTVStatus.

Named VI data and bss/sbss objects including timing, taps, shdwRegs, HorVer, flushFlag, retraceQueue, encoderType, displayOffsetH, displayOffsetV, changeMode, changed, shdwChangeMode, shdwChanged, CurrTiming, CurrTvMode, and message.
Converted a block of VI debug PAL warning bytes from raw .4byte constants into readable .asciz strings with .balign 4 between strings.
Replaced NULL entries in axDspSlave DSP code data with literal .4byte 0x00000000.

Adjusted data declarations and padding in several places, including SendCount as a byte, FIO gSizeOfBlocks using .balign 4 after a byte, FIO gLastErr as a one-byte skip followed by alignment, and removing anonymous padding words after named globals.
smart_moves: Propagated each recovered label through all assembly references instead of only changing the defining label, preserving searchable call/data relationships across files.
Split anonymous aggregate storage only where the diff showed stable offsets and sizes, making later C struct or array recovery easier without changing addresses.
Used explicit alignment directives after byte-sized objects and strings, which keeps symbol boundaries readable while preserving the following labels' expected alignment.
Turned raw ASCII encoded as .4byte values into .asciz strings in VI, making embedded SDK diagnostic text searchable and easier to compare against known Dolphin SDK sources.
Updated external call sites in asm/melee/text_2.s and asm/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.s when SDK functions were renamed, avoiding stale func_ references outside the renamed module.
decomp_lessons: SDK symbol recovery is useful even before C conversion: replacing func_/lbl_ names in assembly makes cross-module behavior and future matching work substantially easier to search.
When naming a small-data or bss symbol, verify the actual storage width; byte-sized flags/counters should not remain as broad anonymous .4byte regions if that obscures subsequent labels.
For contiguous SDK data tables, introduce interior labels such as taps or channelInfo only when the offsets remain compatible with existing references and alignment.
Readable .asciz declarations can replace equivalent raw word dumps for string data, but they must preserve padding/alignment to keep following jump tables or globals at the same addresses.
Call-site cleanup matters: once a recovered SDK function has a name, dependent asm files should be updated in the same pass so future grep results are consistent.
naming_conventions: Dolphin SDK-style exported functions use subsystem prefixes and Pascal/camel casing, e.g. DBGReadStatus, DBInitComm, HIOReadAsync, HIOWriteAsync, MCCExit, MCCEnumDevices, MCCGetFreeBlocks, and VIGetDTVStatus.
Callback names use descriptive Callback suffixes: MWCallback, MTRCallback, DBGCallback, ExiCallback, TxCallback, and RxCallback.
Global MCC objects use g-prefixed names where applicable, such as gChannelInfo, gStreamWork, gIsChannelinfoDirty, gSizeOfBlocks, gProcDone, gLastErr, and gMccInitialized.
VI global state names in this pass preserve SDK-like capitalization and abbreviations: shdwRegs, HorVer, CurrTiming, CurrTvMode, FBSet, PreCB, and PostCB.
Recovered names sometimes preserve apparent SDK spelling exactly, including RecvDataLeng rather than normalizing it to RecvDataLength.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace address-derived labels with recovered SDK names at both definition sites and all lis/addi/lwz/stw/bl reference sites.
Use .balign after byte or string declarations to preserve later symbol addresses while exposing the true logical data size.
For known string payloads, prefer .asciz plus alignment over raw .4byte hexadecimal ASCII when the emitted bytes are equivalent.
For DSP data words that are zero values, this PR used explicit .4byte 0x00000000 instead of NULL; the diff provides the change but not an explicit rationale.
Split large anonymous .skip regions into named subregions with exact byte counts, such as gChannelInfo plus gStreamWork/m_szAdapterMode/m_szInitCode/channelInfo, to prepare for struct recovery.

review_feedback.notes: No review comments or rationale were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/dolphin/vi/vi.s to C using timing, taps, VIGetDTVStatus, shdwRegs, or HorVer.
Find later PRs that define structs for gChannelInfo, gStreamWork, m_szAdapterMode, m_szInitCode, or channelInfo in MCC.
Search for RecvDataLeng and EXIInputFlag to see whether DebuggerDriver data names were later moved into headers.
Search for HIOReadAsync and HIOWriteAsync to track HIO C conversion or prototype recovery.
Search for axDspSlave and 0x00000000 to understand why NULL was avoided in DSP code data.
